title: required-proof/v2
description: Evidence that a repository's Required command graph was run to completion against one exact, clean tree. v2 adds a canonical command-graph witness so an independent consumer can reject a passing subset of the Required graph.

schema.description: Contract discriminator. Consumers must reject documents whose schema they do not implement rather than best-effort parse them.

repo.description: Repository this evidence was produced in. The repo name lives in the payload, never in the schema, so the schema file stays byte-identical across repos.

tool_versions.description: Resolved versions of every tool the graph depended on (e.g. rustc, cargo, cargo-deny).

command_graph.description: Canonical sorted Required command IDs plus the SHA-256 of their compact JSON representation. Consumers require records to match this witness exactly so an omitted gate cannot be hidden behind a passing verdict.

commands.description: One record per command in the Required graph. An empty list is rejected: a proof with no commands is not a proof.

verdict.description: Declared overall verdict. Cross-checked against the command records; a declared pass that the records do not support is rejected (E_VERDICT_MISMATCH).

id.description: Stable identifier for this command within the graph (e.g. fmt, clippy, test-workspace).

tier.description: required: must pass for the proof to pass; a skip here is a failure (E_SKIPPED_AS_PASS). advisory: reported, never gating.

argv.description: Exact argument vector executed, unsplit. Records the command as run, not a prose description of it.

sha.description: Tree the command actually ran against. Must equal source.sha; a differing value means a stale result was carried forward (E_STALE_SHA).

outcome.description: Tri-state. skip is a first-class outcome and must carry a machine-readable reason; it is never silently promoted to pass.

skip_reason.description: Required when outcome is skip. Machine-readable token (e.g. no-live-database), not free prose.

exit_code.description: Process exit status, or null when the command did not run to completion (skipped or interrupted).

ended_at.description: Completion instant, or null if the command never finished. Nullable on purpose: an unfinished command must be representable so it can be rejected with a precise reason (E_UNFINISHED_COMMAND) rather than a generic type error.

resourceBudget.description: Declared resource ceilings the run was executed under. Required so a proof cannot be produced by a run that silently exhausted the host (the retrospective's ~40GB-RSS and 9,700-thread incidents).

isolated_target_dir.description: Per-run build directory. Must not be the shared/global target dir: a shared dir makes a run's inputs unattributable.

pid_task_max.description: Task/PID ceiling. The scarce resource in the cgroup-exhaustion incident; a memory cap alone was proven insufficient.

nullableTimestamp.description: An RFC 3339 UTC instant, or null when the event has not happened. Null is representable so it can be rejected by a named semantic rule rather than a generic type error.

sha1.description: Full 40-character lowercase hex git object name. Abbreviated or uppercase SHAs are rejected: evidence must name an exact, unambiguous commit.

sourceRef.description: The exact tree this evidence was produced from.

tree_clean.description: False means uncommitted changes were present. Evidence from a dirty tree is not reproducible and is rejected (E_TREE_DIRTY).
